sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Philip, presented with a one-week history of right elbow pain that began after playing lacrosse with his children.
He reports that the pain is sharp and radiates down his forearm, worsening with movement of the elbow.
He denies any history of falling but mentions that his son was checking him several times during the game, which may have contributed to the injury.
He has tried taking Advil, which provided some relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes.
He is currently on 20mg of lisinopril for hypertension and 500mg of metformin for diabetes.
He reports following a Mediterranean diet, which is low in salt.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was 150/90, which is high. [NOT MENTIONED for pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature]
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear bilaterally, and the heart exam reveals a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, which has been present before.
The right elbow exam shows pain to palpation of the olecranon area of the posterior elbow, mild pain with flexion and extension, and normal range of motion.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a right elbow contusion, likely caused by a lacrosse stick check from his son, resulting in pain and swelling.
Problem list:
Elbow contusion; Hypertension; Diabetes; Systolic ejection murmur (past/confirmed)
Plan:
I prescribed 800mg of ibuprofen twice daily for the elbow contusion to help with pain and swelling.
I instructed the patient to ice the elbow three times a day for 20 minutes each time.
I advised the patient to avoid playing lacrosse with his son for the next couple of weeks to allow the elbow to heal.
I will continue the patient on 20mg of lisinopril daily for hypertension and 500mg of metformin daily for diabetes.
We will also obtain an A1c to check the patient's blood sugar levels and adjust the metformin dosage as needed.
Follow-up: We will see the patient next time, and the nurse will bring in the prescriptions.
